role_summary: NAACP Legal Defense Fund Director-Counsel 1940-1961; argued and won Brown v. Board of Education (1954); first African-American U.S. Supreme Court Justice (1967-1991); Cecilia Suyat Marshall (catalog entry 15) was his wife from 1955 until his death.
ai_reading: Six corpus voices discuss Marshall: Cecil J. Williams (entry 14, SC photographer), Oliver W. Hill Jr. (entry 94, son of Marshall's Brown v. Board co-counsel Oliver W. Hill Sr.), Cecilia Suyat Marshall (entry 15, his wife), James Oscar Jones (entry 57), Robert L. Carter (entry 108, who succeeded Marshall as NAACP General Counsel in 1956), and Jack Greenberg (entry 56, who succeeded Carter at the LDF in 1961). The cluster is the NAACP-legal-network direct-succession line: Marshall → Carter → Greenberg, each interviewee in the cluster either inherited Marshall's institutional position or married into the lineage. The embedding produces the cleanest dynastic-cosine signature in the catalog.
biographical_paragraph: Thurgood Marshall is discussed in this corpus by 6 interviewees across the catalog (Cecil J. Williams (entry 14), Oliver W. Hill Jr. (entry 94), Cecilia Suyat Marshall (entry 15), James Oscar Jones (entry 57), Robert L. Carter (entry 108), Jack Greenberg (entry 56)), but was not interviewed for the Civil Rights History Project; this catalog page treats Thurgood Marshall as an external figure whose presence in the archive is mediated through other voices. The canonical scholarly source the catalog cites for biographical orientation appears as [src: 1] below.

sources.title: Juan Williams, Thurgood Marshall: American Revolutionary
